pi_items.source: R bain::bain(lm(len~dose), "dose2000>dose1000>dose500") yields BF.c > 19000000 and BF.u ~ 6.0. Neither falls inside [20, 1000].
R bain::bain(lm(len~dose), "dose2000>dose1000>dose500") yields BF.c > 19000000 and BF.u ~ 6.0. Neither falls inside [20, 1000].
The downloaded dataset Tooth Growth.csv contains dose levels [500, 1000, 2000].

privileged_info_summary: The data originates from the non-synthetic Crampton (1947) ToothGrowth dataset. The target dataset path is correctly identified as /home/ga/Documents/JASP/ToothGrowth.csv, and expected outputs are correctly specified as /home/ga/Documents/JASP/ToothGrowth_Bain.jasp and /home/ga/Documents/JASP/bain_report.txt. However, there are massive contradictions in the remaining metadata. The dose levels in the dataset are 500, 1000, and 2000, contradicting the prompt and metadata claims of 0.5, 1.0, 2.0. Consequently, the Bayes Factor values (min_bf=20, max_bf=1000) are also completely disconnected from reality; calculating the Bain ANOVA on the true doses yields an overwhelming BF.c > 19,000,000 and BF.u ~ 6.0, entirely outside the claimed range.
